**CONSULTING SERVICES AGREEMENT REQUIRED ONLY FOR FIRST ENGAGEMENT. SUBSEQUENT PROJECT WILL BE SOW ONLY.**

**CONSULTING SERVICES AGREEMENT**

This Consulting Services Agreement, dated as of \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ (the “Effective Date”), is between BHS Consulting LLC, dba Sayer, a Texas limited liability company (“Sayer”) with a principal place of business of 1901 N. Akard St. Dallas, TX, 75201 and \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ (“Client”), with a principal place of business at  \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_.

Client desires to engage Sayer to provide certain management consulting services on the terms and conditions set forth in this Consulting Services Agreement and any schedules, exhibits or appendices hereto (collectively, the “CSA” or the “Agreement”).

In consideration of the mutual covenants set forth in this Agreement and intending to be legally bound, the parties agree as follows: 

\+ ARTICLE I   
\+ DEFINITIONS

1.01 Definitions. In this CSA, unless the contrary intention appears, capitalized terms shall have the meaning set forth below: 

“Affiliate” means an entity that owns or controls, is owned or controlled by, or is under common control or ownership with a Party, where “control” is defined as the possession, direct or indirect, of the power to direct or cause the direction of the management and policies of an entity, whether through ownership of voting securities, by contract or otherwise. 

“Confidential Information” has the meaning set out in Section 3.04. 

“Effective Date” is the date of commencement of this CSA listed in the introductory paragraph. 

“Input” means any documentation, materials or information provided by one Party to the other under a Work Order. 

“CSA” means this Consulting Services Agreement. 

“Party” means a party to this CSA. 

“Services” means management consulting services, provision of training and any other services that are the subject matter of a Statement of Work.

“Statement of Work” means an individual agreement between Sayer and Client for the provision of one or more specific Service(s). 

“Term” means the period of time that this Agreement is in effect, including the initial term and any extensions or renewals thereof. 

“Training” means study programs, courses or training. 

“Work Product” means the results of the Services. 

\+ ARTICLE II   
\+ STATEMENT OF WORK 

2.01 Framework. This Agreement establishes a framework by which Sayer may contract with Client to provide services. The specific Services that Sayer will provide to Client will be described in one or more mutually agreed upon Statements of Work signed by Sayer and Client. Each Statement of Work shall be treated as a separate agreement under the terms of this CSA. Each Statement of Work shall include, to the extent applicable, the following items: (a) a description of the Services to be performed, (b) any deliverables and/or milestones to be prepared or completed by Sayer or its contractors (as identified in an applicable Statement of Work), (c) the tasks and resources Sayer and/or Client will provide, and (d) pricing and payment terms as set forth in Exhibit A, the Statement of Work Template. 

2.02 Additional Work. In the event that Client requests additional work not included on a particular Statement of Work, Client shall be responsible for additional fees at Sayer’s usual rates for the type of work requested. Such additional work shall be invoiced in accordance with the payment schedule for the applicable Statement of Work. Further, Client agrees that additional work requests may extend or delay any deliverables or deadlines previously agreed upon in a Statement of Work. 

2.03 Affiliates. Sayer and Client agree that the Affiliates of Sayer may provide Services for Client or its Affiliates by entering into a Statement of Work with Sayer that incorporates the terms and conditions of this Agreement. The parties acknowledge that adjustments to the terms of this Agreement may be made by means of a particular Statement of Work, e.g., to address flow down provisions, disparate tax, and/or legal regimes in other geographies. 

\+ ARTICLE III   
\+ CONDITIONS APPLICABLE TO SERVICE 

3.01 General Terms. Sayer shall provide, and Client shall receive, the Services in accordance with this CSA and the terms of the relevant Statement of Work. 

3.02 Replacement of Person(s). If a Statement of Work has been entered into with the expectation of performance by a particular person, Sayer shall have the right to replace such person by one or more other persons, subject to consultation with Client, provided that the replacement(s) have the necessary qualifications. 

3.03 Working on Client’s Premises. In the event that Sayer’s employees perform work on Client’s premises, Client shall provide all facilities that are reasonably desired by those employees, free of charge, including but not limited to an appropriate working space with computer and telecommunication facilities. The working space and facilities shall comply with all applicable statutory and other requirements and provisions concerning working conditions where the work is performed. 

3.04 Confidential Information. Sayer acknowledges and agrees that any and all information of whatever kind or nature relating to this Agreement, or which Sayer acquires in connection with or as a result of this Agreement, or which Sayer receives or becomes aware of during the term of this Agreement, in each case concerning Client is confidential and proprietary (“Confidential Information”). Confidential Information does not include any information which is in the public domain through no breach of this Agreement or information learned by Sayer from a third party who to Sayer’s knowledge after due inquiry does not possess such information unlawfully. Sayer will use Confidential Information only for the purposes of this Agreement, and will not duplicate it, use it any time to provide, perform or furnish any services or to invent, create, modify, adapt or manufacture any products that would compete with or be used in lieu of Client’s services or products, or disclose it to a third party unless disclosure is approved in advance in writing by Client. Sayer will limit access to Confidential Information to only those of its full-time employees who have need for such access to perform this Agreement and a Statement of Work and will ensure that any such employees are aware of and agree in writing to comply with this Article. Sayer shall at all times comply with all applicable laws and regulations in connection with its processing, use, access, disclosure, and storage of Confidential Information. Sayer implements and maintains security procedures and measures designed to preserve the security and confidentiality of Confidential Information processed, accessed, and stored by it and protects Confidential Information against unauthorized and unlawful disclosure, access, or processing, accidental loss, destruction or damage. Sayer shall maintain physical and technical security to protect the use, security, confidentiality, and integrity of Confidential Information and the infrastructure used to store and process the same, whether in electronic or physical form, including but not limited to IT restricted space, computer facilities, telecommunications/local area networks, media storage, and any facility or area in which Confidential Information is physically handled. Upon becoming aware of a security breach with respect to Sayer’s or a third party’s data storage system or services which has or would be reasonably likely to have any effect on Confidential Information, or of circumstances that are reasonably understood to suggest such a security breach is likely, Sayer will immediately notify Client, and, at its own expense, fully investigate the incident and cooperate fully with Client’s reasonable investigation of and response to the incident. Except as otherwise required by law, Sayer will not provide notice of the incident directly to individuals whose personally identifiable information was involved, regulatory agencies, or other entities, without the prior written permission from Client, such notice to be provided at Sayer’s expense. Client will have no obligation to keep secret or maintain in confidence and may use without restriction for its own or others’ benefit, any information that receives or becomes aware of from Sayer or its employees. 

3.05 Independent Contractor. Sayer and its contractors shall perform this Agreement as independent contractors, and, as such, shall have and maintain sole control over all of its employees, agents, operations, and Services. Neither Sayer or its contractors, nor any of their respective employees or agents shall represent, act, report to act, or be deemed to be the agent, representative, employee, or servant of Client. The parties acknowledge that any Services rendered to Client are and have been provided with the understanding that Sayer and its contractors are independent contractors for payroll tax reporting purposes. Accordingly, Sayer and its contractors agree that they will be responsible for employment tax and other required taxes in connection with compensation for Services provided under this Agreement. 

3.06 Request of Client Input. At Sayer’s request, Client will provide, at Client’s own cost, Sayer with all Input and cooperation required in order to complete the Services. Client acknowledges that the start date and the end date of the work described in a Statement of Work depends on the timely receipt of Input from Client and the performance of Client’s cooperation obligations. In case of any delay therein, Sayer’s obligations under the relevant Statement of Work will be suspended for the period of the delay. 

3.07 Marketing Materials. Client gives Sayer permission to use logo and Client legal name in internal and external marketing materials, including Sayer’s company website, in accordance with Client’s usage guidelines. Client gives Sayer permission to prepare case studies documenting the value that Sayer delivered throughout any engagements executed within the scope of this agreement. 

3.08 Assurance and Certification. In the performance of the work, in accordance with the governing Statement of Work herein or subsequent Statements of Work, Sayer and the Client agree that deliverables sent by Sayer, information compiled by Sayer (in any form), client information (in any form) or consultative advice communicated by Sayer shall not represent assurance or certification. Assurance and certification of such client information is the responsibility of the Client, not Sayer. Sayer is engaged to provide consultative advice only and is not engaged to provide assurance or certification in any form by any means in performance of the Statement of Work. Further, any interpretation of the consultative advice given by Sayer is the independent conclusion of the Client. 

\+ ARTICLE IV   
\+ COMPENSATION RATES AND PAYMENTS 

4.01 Payment Basis and Reimbursement of Expenses. Client will pay Sayer in accordance with the payment details in the applicable Statement of Work. Sayer may adjust or modify rates at any time during the term of the Agreement, provided such modified rate is documented in the applicable Statement of Work before the commencement of the Services. Pre-approved expenses are reimbursable in accordance with the terms of, and only to the extent provided for in, a Statement of Work. Sayer shall keep records adequate to verify each statement of expenses. All expenses will comply with the Sayer’s Travel and Expense Policy as provided and identified in Exhibit B of this Agreement. Sayer will charge fees, as specified in a Statement of Work, that are payable depending on the terms selected by the Client in the Statement of Work. 

4.02 Payment Default. If Client fails to pay Sayer any payment owed when due, Client shall be in default of this CSA, and Sayer shall be entitled to cease service immediately, as specified in Section 4.04 of this agreement. In addition, the client will owe the outstanding invoice amount plus a late charge equal to five percent (5%) of the outstanding invoice amount. 

4.03 Right of Offset. Client shall have no right to offset, deduct, or withhold payment for any reason unless Sayer breaches this Agreement. 

4.04 Effect of Delay on Performance of Work. If Client payment does not clear for any outstanding amount or if Client fails to pay any full-rate invoiced amount within payment terms as specified in a Statement of Work, Sayer is entitled to suspend any performance under a Statement of Work with Client, or reduce the resources specified in the Statement of Work in order to avoid further loss. Once all invoices are paid in full, Sayer is entitled to choose to either restart or to terminate the uncompleted Statement of Work. Sayer SHALL BEAR NO LIABILITY FOR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES OR LOST PROFITS SHOULD SUCH SUSPENSION OR TERMINATION OF WORK UNDER THIS SECTION 4.04 CAUSE ANY LOSS TO CLIENT. 

\+ ARTICLE V   
\+ TERM AND TERMINATION 

5.01 Term. This Agreement shall commence as of the Effective Date and shall continue for a period of two ( 2 ) years. Upon expiration, this Agreement shall automatically renew for successive one ( 1 ) year periods unless otherwise terminated in accordance with this Article V. Either Party may forego the automatic renewal by providing written notice to the other Party at least thirty (30) days prior to the expiration of the current Term. Notwithstanding the foregoing, in the event that any Statement of Work remains in effect following such termination or expiration, this Agreement shall govern and continue in effect with regard to such Statement of Work until the termination of such Statement of Work. 

5.02 Termination of Agreement. Either Party may terminate this Agreement: 

(a)for convenience with thirty (30) days prior written notice to the other Party. In such an event, each Statement of Work executed by the parties prior to the effective date of such termination shall remain in full force and effect in accordance with its terms, including the terms and conditions of this Agreement as necessary to perform the Statement of Work. 

(b)if either Party shall at any time default in the payment of any fee or commit any breach of any promise or agreement contained in any Section of this Agreement, and shall fail to remedy any such default or breach within thirty (30) days after written notice by the non-breaching Party. 

5.03 Statement of Work Termination. Sayer may terminate a Statement of Work or this Agreement for cause with ten (10) days’ written notice to Client if Client commits a material breach, other than non-payment which shall be governed by Section 4.04, of the Statement of Work and such breach is not cured within such ten (10) day period. 

5.04 Obligations. Upon the reasonable written request of Client, Sayer will return, or with the written consent of Client, destroy Client’s Confidential Information and all data, materials, and other property held by Sayer in connection with the performance of Services under this Agreement and shall work together in good faith to address any and all post-termination issues concerning this Agreement. Any and all unpaid fees and expenses incurred in connection with the Services prior to termination shall become due and payable according to the applicable payment terms of this Agreement and/or an applicable Statement of Work. 

5.05 Effects of Termination. Each Party’s obligations under Article IV, Article VII, Article VIII, and Sections 3.04 shall explicitly survive expiration or termination of this Agreement. 

\+ ARTICLE VI   
\+ WARRANTIES 

6.01 Warranties. Sayer warrants that: ( 1 ) the Services will be performed in a timely and professional manner by qualified personnel; ( 2 ) deliverables and materials prepared by Sayer and its employees will conform to the requirements of this Agreement and the relevant Statement of Work; ( 3 ) the performance of the Services and the use or resale of deliverables and materials furnished by Sayer under a Statement of Work will not infringe or otherwise violate any copyright, trade secret, trademark, patent, or other right of any third party and will comply with all applicable governmental laws, rules, orders, and regulations; and ( 4 ) entering into this Agreement and the performance of Services does not violate the terms and conditions of any other agreement or contract or any legal obligation. THE WARRANTIES IN THIS ARTICLE VI AND ANY WARRANTY IN A STATEMENT OF WORK ARE IN LIEU OF ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, OR WHETHER ARISING BY COURSE OF DEALING OR PERFORMANCE, CUSTOM, USAGE IN THE TRADE OR PROFESSION OR OTHERWISE, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO, IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY AND FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. 

\+ ARTICLE VII   
\+ LIMITATION OF LIABILITY 

7.01Limitation of Sayer’s Liability. 

(a)Reserved 

(b)NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE FOR ANY SPECIAL, INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES OF ANY KIND WHATSOEVER (INCLUDING, WITHOUT LIMITATION, ATTORNEYS’ FEES) IN ANY WAY DUE TO, RESULTING FROM OR ARISING IN CONNECTION WITH ANY OF THE SERVICES OR THE PERFORMANCE OF OR FAILURE TO PERFORM SUCH PARTY’S OBLIGATIONS UNDER THIS CSA. THIS DISCLAIMER APPLIES WITHOUT LIMITATION (I) TO CLAIMS ARISING FROM THE PROVISION OF THE SERVICES OR ANY FAILURE OR DELAY IN CONNECTION THEREWITH; (II) TO CLAIMS FOR LOST PROFITS; (III) REGARDLESS OF THE FORM OF ACTION, WHETHER IN CONTRACT, TORT (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE), STRICT LIABILITY, OR OTHERWISE; AND (IV) REGARDLESS OF WHETHER SUCH DAMAGES ARE FORESEEABLE OR WHETHER SUCH PARTY HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES. 

(c) EACH PARTY’S LIABILITY FOR DAMAGES HEREUNDER SHALL IN NO EVENT EXCEED IN THE AGGREGATE OF THE AMOUNT OF FEES PAID AND OWED BY CLIENT UNDER THIS AGREEMENT FOR THE IMMEDIATELY PRECEDING TWELVE MONTH PERIOD. THE PARTIES HAVE AGREED THAT THESE LIMITATIONS WILL SURVIVE AND APPLY EVEN IF ANY LIMITED REMEDY SPECIFIED IN THIS AGREEMENT IS FOUND TO HAVE FAILED OF ITS ESSENTIAL PURPOSE. 

(d)Client agrees that it shall, in all circumstances, use commercially reasonable efforts to mitigate and otherwise minimize its damages, whether direct or indirect, due to, resulting from or arising in connection with any failure by Sayer to comply fully with its obligations under this CSA. 

7.02 Indemnification of Client by Sayer. Sayer agrees to indemnify and hold harmless Client and its respective directors, officers, agents and employees (each, a “Client Indemnified Person”) from and against any damages, and shall reimburse each Client Indemnified Person for all reasonable expenses (including reasonable attorney’s fees) as they are incurred in investigating, preparing or defending any claim, action, proceeding, or investigation, whether or not in connection with pending or threatened litigation and whether or not any Client Indemnified Person is a party, arising out of or in connection with any breach of this agreement, or any negligent acts or misconduct by Sayer or its contractors in performance of the Services. Despite the foregoing, Sayer shall not be responsible for any damages of any Client Indemnified Person that have resulted from the gross negligence or willful misconduct of such or any Client Indemnified Person in connection with any of the advice, actions, inactions, or Services referred to above. 

7.03Indemnification of Sayer by Client. Client agrees to indemnify and hold harmless Sayer and its respective directors, officers, agents, employees, and contractors (each an “Sayer Indemnified Person”) from and against any damages, and shall reimburse each Sayer Indemnified Person for all reasonable expenses (including reasonable attorney’s fees) as they are incurred in investigating, preparing or defending any claim, action, proceeding, or investigation, whether or not in connection with pending or threatened litigation and whether or not any Sayer Indemnified Person is a party, arising out of or in connection with any breach of this agreement, or any negligent acts or misconduct by Client. Despite the foregoing, Client shall not be responsible for any damages of any Sayer Indemnified Person that have resulted from the gross negligence or willful misconduct of such Sayer Indemnified Person in connection with any of the advice, actions, inactions, or Services referred to above. 

\+ ARTICLE VIII   
\+ GENERAL TERMS 

8.01 Choice of Law; Venue. This Agreement shall be construed under and in accordance with the laws of the State of Texas except for its conflicts of laws provisions. Venue for any dispute between the parties will be in the state or federal courts of Dallas County, Texas. 

8.02 Assignment. Client may not assign its rights or delegate its obligations hereunder without the written consent of Sayer 

8.03 Notification. Notices must be in writing, and shall be considered to have been received when delivered by hand, received via electronic email, or five (5) days after sent using a delivery service similar to USPS Certified Mail to the addresses below: 

If to Client: 

If to Sayer: 1901 N. Akard St., Dallas, Texas 75214

8.04 Remedies. Remedies for breach are cumulative and may be exercised separately or concurrently. The exercise of one remedy does not exclude any other remedies that the parties are entitled to receive. The remedies provided in this Agreement shall not affect remedies otherwise available at law or in equity. 

8.05 Entire Agreement, Amendment, Severability and Waiver. This Agreement, including its attachments and Statements of Work, constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof. All prior agreements, understandings and proposals, oral or written, between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof are null and void and superseded by this Agreement. This Agreement may only be modified or amended by a writing signed by both parties. If any provision of this Agreement is determined to be invalid or unenforceable in whole or in part, the remaining provisions will continue in full force and effect. No waiver of any breach or default hereunder shall be deemed a waiver of any subsequent breach or default of the same or similar nature. No provision of the Agreement will be waived and no breach excused unless the waiver or consent is in writing and is signed by the Party that is claimed to have waived or consented. To the extent of any express conflict or inconsistency between the terms and conditions of a Statement of Work and the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the terms and conditions of this Agreement will control, except with respect to any terms relating to the Services to be performed under a Statement of Work (wherein the terms and conditions of the Statement of Work will control solely with respect to such Services). 

8.06 Force Majeure. Neither Party shall be responsible for delays or failures in performance resulting from causes beyond the reasonable control and without the fault or negligence of such Party and/or its permitted contractors. Such acts shall include but not be limited to acts of God, riots, acts of war, epidemics, governmental regulations superimposed after the fact, fire, communication line failures, power failures, earthquakes, floods or other natural disasters or acts or omissions of Client or Client’s failure to perform in a satisfactory and timely fashion as required by this Agreement and/or Client Contract (the “Force Majeure Event”). Delays in delivery or in meeting completion dates due to Force Majeure Events shall automatically extend such dates (and the term of this Agreement or the applicable Statement of Work, if appropriate) for a period equal to the duration of such events, provided that the Party whose performance has been impaired on account of a Force Majeure Event has given immediate written notice to the other Party of the occurrence and estimated impact of such Force Majeure Event. If a Force Majeure Event should last more than sixty (60) days, the Party whose performance has not been impaired on account of the Force Majeure Event may, at its option, elect to terminate this Agreement without further liability to either Party. 

8.07 Non-solicitation. Client agrees not to solicit or hire any personnel of Sayer with whom Client has had contact in connection with any Statement of Work during the term of and for one ( 1 ) year after termination or expiration of such Statement of Work; provided that Client may hire an individual employed by Sayer who, without other solicitation, responds to advertisements or solicitations aimed at the general public. If, during the course of this Agreement or for one ( 1 ) year after termination or expiration of an applicable Statement of Work, Client or one of its affiliates does hire personnel of Sayer, a fee of one-hundred percent (100%) of such personnel’s first year annual salary (including any guaranteed compensation, salary, and/or signing bonus) will be due and payable to Sayer within thirty (30) days of hire, unless otherwise agreed upon by the Parties. 

8.08 Dispute Resolution. Any controversies arising out of the terms of this CSA or its interpretation or a Statement of Work shall be first attempted to be settled by an independent third party mediator determined by mutual agreement between the parties. Any matter not yet resolved shall be settled in Texas in accordance with the rules of the American Arbitration Association, and the judgment upon award may be entered in any court having jurisdiction thereof. 

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, each of the parties hereto has executed this Agreement by its duly authorized representatives effective as of the Effective Date.

**\+ STATEMENT OF WORK** 

Client: HelloSpoke

Tentative Project Start Date: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

**1\. Executive Summary**   
HelloSpoke is migrating from Salesforce to HubSpot to centralize CRM, quoting, and post-sale handoffs in one platform. Jeremy has already built the foundational data pipeline — Salesforce and Rev IO data are loaded into a HubSpot sandbox, the ClickUp integration is wired, and ALN is being finalized in-house. Sayer’s engagement is to audit and validate that foundation, rebuild the quoting workflow on Commerce Hub CPQ (Jeremy’s #1 priority), define and gate the sales pipeline, configure the automations and dashboards that make the system useful day-to-day, refine the ClickUp ↔ HubSpot workflow, and train the team to own it. This Statement of Work is a 12-week, time-and-materials engagement billed at $150/hour across ten workstreams plus project management, with a fixed not-to-exceed cap of $24,600.

**2\. Objectives** 

* Stand up an opinionated HubSpot foundation — objects, pipelines, lead status, lifecycle stages, properties, team permissions — aligned to how HelloSpoke actually sells, implements, and renews   
* Independently audit the data Jeremy has loaded into the sandbox and deliver a production-push green-light memo before any record goes live   
* Replace the CSV-driven quoting pain with Commerce Hub CPQ: product library, quote templates, validation, approvals, native e-signature on quotes, and quote-to-deal automation   
* Define sales-stage definitions with Sara and enforce required-field gating at the sales → implementation and implementation → billing handoffs   
* Build the automations and three role-based dashboards (executive, sales rep, ops) that drive day-to-day visibility    
* Refine the ClickUp ↔ HubSpot workflow Jeremy already built — status alignment, gating, who-does-what — without rebuilding the integration   
* Replace DocuSign for quote signing via Commerce Hub native e-signature; evaluate PandaDoc as the backup for non-quote contracts   
* Train admins and end users on the new system and deliver a clean UAT-validated go-live with 30-day hypercare

**3\. Scope of Work**

The engagement is structured in three sequential phases plus a cross-phase project management workstream. All hours are estimates at $150/hour; total estimated fee is **$24,600 (164 hours)** as a not-to-exceed cap. Client will only be billed for actual hours worked. Detailed time sheets will be provided with each invoice.

#### Phase 1: Foundation + Audit — Weeks 1-4 (46 hours)

**Workstream 1: HubSpot Foundational Architecture (18 hours)**

*Customer Story.* HelloSpoke is migrating from Salesforce to HubSpot. The CRM only delivers value if its bones — objects, pipelines, lead status, lifecycle stages, properties — are configured to match how HelloSpoke actually sells, implements, and renews. The team needs an opinionated foundation, not a blank canvas.

*Recommended Approach.*
→ Validate HubSpot tier (Sales Hub Professional minimum; Commerce Hub Professional required for W4)   
→ Configure standard objects (Companies, Contacts, Deals); review and validate custom objects from Jeremy’s sandbox   
→ Build sales pipeline with Sara’s finalized stages; configure lead status values and lifecycle stage progression rules   
→ Property strategy: required vs optional, snake\_case naming, group organization, archival of redundant Salesforce-era properties   
→ Team and permission structure aligned with sales, ops, and leadership roles   
→ Document architecture decisions in the admin playbook   

*Assumptions.*
→ HelloSpoke is on Sales Hub Professional or Enterprise (validated during kickoff)   
→ Object/property catalog from Jeremy’s sandbox is available for reference   
→ Sara delivers finalized sales-stage definitions before W1 build starts   
→ One sales pipeline and one implementation/onboarding pipeline; no multi-currency or multi-region complexity

**Workstream 2: Data QA + Pre-Production Audit (18 hours)**

*Customer Story.* Jeremy has already pulled Salesforce and Rev IO data into the HubSpot sandbox. Before any of it goes to production, HelloSpoke needs an independent QA review — someone who didn’t build it — to validate data integrity, association coverage, and that nothing will break the moment users start touching records.

*Recommended Approach.*
→ Sandbox access and initial inventory (record counts, object coverage, data age)   
→ Data integrity audit: completeness, dedupe candidates, formatting consistency, null-rate review on critical fields   
→ Object and property mapping validation: source-to-target mapping correctness, no orphaned values, type coercion verified   
→ Association validation: Contact ↔ Company ↔ Deal coverage plus custom-object associations   
→ Custom-object structure review: schema correctness, association labels, pipeline alignment   
→ Error handling and rollback plan: documented cutover sequence, fail-safe checkpoints, rollback procedure   
→ Production-push green-light memo to Jeremy   

*Assumptions.*
→ Jeremy grants Sayer access to the HubSpot sandbox before W2 begins   
→ Data is already loaded into sandbox; Sayer is auditing, not migrating   
→ Rev IO custom-object schema already built; Sayer validates, does not redesign   
→ Up to 2 rounds of audit findings plus remediation; further rounds are change orders   
→ Sayer is not responsible for the original migration build or for fixing pre-existing source-system data quality issues — only for surfacing them and recommending remediation

**Workstream 3: Sales Stage Definitions + Gating (10 hours)**

*Customer Story.* Sales reps need a pipeline that matches how HelloSpoke actually sells, with handoffs to implementation and billing that don’t drop information. Jeremy has been clear: required fields enforced at the right gates so deals can’t advance without the data the next team needs.

*Recommended Approach.*
→ Workshop with Sara and Christina to lock final sales-stage definitions   
→ Build stages in the HubSpot sales pipeline with agreed names, probabilities, and stage-entry/exit criteria   
→ Configure required-field gating at two handoffs: Sales → Implementation (up to 10 required fields) and Implementation → Billing (up to 10 required fields)   
→ Workflow validation that surfaces the specific missing field to the rep when advance is attempted   
→ Stuck-deal HubSpot report for leadership showing records blocked at each gate   
→ Document gating rules in the admin playbook   

*Assumptions.*
→ Sara delivers finalized sales-stage definitions in week 1 of delivery   
→ Up to 10 required fields per gate, 2 gates total   
→ Hard-block (workflow-enforced) governance, not soft-warn — confirmed with Jeremy at workshop

#### Phase 2: Build — Weeks 5-9 (80 hours)

**Workstream 4: Quoting & CPQ Workflow — Commerce Hub (24 hours)**

*Customer Story.* Jeremy’s #1 named priority. Reps today struggle to build quotes because the product CSV upload is slow and error-prone. HelloSpoke wants Commerce Hub CPQ to absorb the quote-to-cash workflow with native e-signature on quotes — no DocuSign required for the quote-signing step.

*Recommended Approach.*
→ Commerce Hub Professional setup and Commerce Hub seat assignment for the quoting team (tier validation in W1)   
→ Product library architecture: SKU structure, pricing rules, product groups   
→ CSV product upload pattern: standard product import workflow plus bulk-update via product ID mapping (tier-priced products require manual creation per HubSpot constraint)   
→ Quote template configuration with HelloSpoke branding   
→ Validation rules and approval workflows for quotes above pricing thresholds   
→ Native e-signature on quotes (Dropbox Sign-powered; included with Commerce Hub Professional). Cap awareness: 25 signatures per user per month on Pro, 50 per user on Enterprise — pooled across users   
→ Quote-to-deal automation: quote acceptance triggers deal stage advance plus downstream task creation   
→ Quoting documentation and handoff into W9 training   

*Assumptions.*
→ HelloSpoke is on, or upgrading to, Commerce Hub Professional minimum   
→ Quote signature volume fits within pooled Commerce Hub Pro caps; Enterprise upgrade is a client-side licensing decision if volume exceeds   
→ Existing product catalog provided in CSV format with consistent SKU and pricing structure   
→ No multi-currency quotes   
→ Native HubSpot e-signature replaces DocuSign for quote signing only; non-quote contracts are addressed in W5

**Workstream 5: DocuSign Replacement Evaluation + Setup (6 hours)**

*Customer Story.* Jeremy is killing DocuSign. The replacement decision hinges on whether HelloSpoke needs to sign anything that isn’t a quote. If not, Commerce Hub native signature in W4 is the full answer and W5 absorbs minimal hours. If yes, PandaDoc (HubSpot-native) is the backup for general contracts (SOWs, MSAs, NDAs).

*Recommended Approach.*
→ Decision-support workshop with Jeremy: catalog non-quote signing needs (volumes per year, document types)   
→ If Commerce Hub native suffices (quote-only signing): extended config beyond W4 — signature delegation rules, branded signing experience, audit-trail configuration   
→ If PandaDoc selected: account setup (client-billed), HubSpot ↔ PandaDoc native integration installation, template migration for top 5 document types, deal-stage automation linking signed-doc events to HubSpot   
→ Audit trail and signing experience verification   

*Assumptions.*
→ W5 hours sized assuming Commerce Hub native is sufficient. If PandaDoc is selected, hours run at the high end of range and may trigger a change order   
→ PandaDoc subscription, if needed, billed under HelloSpoke’s account   
→ Up to 5 document templates migrated if PandaDoc path; further templates are change orders

**Workstream 6: Automations & Workflows (20 hours)**

*Customer Story.* Sales reps and ops need the system to do the chasing — not the other way around. Lead-to-account-to-quote-to-order-to-kickoff should run on rails, with notifications, task creation, and ownership routing happening automatically.

*Recommended Approach.*
→ Deal-stage automations: notifications, task creation on stage advance, property updates   
→ Lifecycle stage progression: lead → MQL → SQL → opportunity → customer transitions tied to deal events   
→ Customer-journey automation: lead capture → company association → quote-ready → order → onboarding kickoff   
→ Sales-team notifications and ownership routing (round-robin or rule-based)   
→ Stuck-deal alerts to managers   
→ Internal email templates plus automated send-on-trigger sequences   

*Assumptions.*
→ Up to 12 workflows in scope; further workflows or branching complexity are change orders   
→ HubSpot Operations Hub not required (standard Sales Hub workflow tools sufficient)   
→ No marketing email nurture flows (covered in a future engagement)

**Workstream 7: Reporting & Dashboards (18 hours)**

*Customer Story.* Jeremy has been emphatic — the system isn’t useful if dashboards don’t show the right things in the right places. Three audiences (executive, sales rep, ops) need their own views with the metrics they actually use.

*Recommended Approach.*
→ Executive dashboard: revenue, pipeline health, conversion rates, deal velocity   
→ Sales rep dashboard: my deals, my activity, my targets, stuck-deal alerts   
→ Ops dashboard: onboarding pipeline view, ClickUp status sync (read-only), customer health   
→ Custom views and saved filters per role (sales rep, sales leader, ops, exec) — 4 to 6 views   
→ Report templates and scheduled-export configuration   

*Assumptions.*
→ Up to 3 dashboards (one per audience), 5–8 reports per dashboard   
→ HubSpot reporting tier sufficient (Sales Hub Pro minimum)   
→ Custom report builder used; no external BI tool integration in scope   
→ Sales rep targets and quotas data provided by HelloSpoke

**Workstream 8: ClickUp Workflow Refinement (12 hours)**

*Customer Story.* The ClickUp ↔ HubSpot integration already exists — Jeremy built it. What HelloSpoke needs now is workflow correctness: the right people doing the right things at the right time. Status alignment, gating, who-does-what.

*Recommended Approach.*
→ Current-state review of Jeremy-built integration and automation (deal-won → ClickUp task)   
→ Workflow correctness audit: status meaningfulness, task routing accuracy, implementation visibility flowing back to sales   
→ Status alignment: confirm canonical onboarding statuses are mirrored in HubSpot for sales-rep visibility   
→ Gating and handoff rules between sales, implementation, and ops   
→ Documentation of who-does-what plus SOPs for the implementation team   
→ Hand-off sync configuration tweaks (NOT integration build — this is configuration of what Jeremy already wired)   

*Assumptions.*
→ ClickUp integration is functional in Jeremy’s sandbox at start of W8 (validated during W2)   
→ ClickUp side: Christina, Dalton, and Haley make ClickUp-side changes; Sayer does not directly modify ClickUp configuration   
→ 10DLC training and Win-property surveys remain decoupled per 4/23 decision

#### Phase 3: Enable + Go-Live — Weeks 10-12 (22 hours)

**Workstream 9: Training & Enablement (12 hours)**

*Customer Story.* Jeremy and Christina won’t get value from the rebuild if the team can’t use it. Training has two tracks: admins (Christina plus designated HelloSpoke admin) need depth; end users need workflow-specific training tied to what they actually do.

*Recommended Approach.*
→ Admin training session 1: CRM structure, workflows, reports (recorded)   
→ Admin training session 2: report building and user management (recorded)   
→ End-user sales training: pipeline workflow, quoting in Commerce Hub, dashboard usage (recorded plus live Q&A)   
→ End-user ops training: onboarding workflow, ClickUp ↔ HubSpot status sync, handoff gates (recorded)   
→ Admin playbook plus end-user quick-reference cards   

*Assumptions.*
→ Up to 2 admin training sessions and 2 end-user sessions   
→ Sessions delivered live and recorded; recordings hosted on HelloSpoke’s side   
→ 10DLC training tracked separately per 4/23 decision

**Workstream 10: UAT & Go-Live (10 hours)**

*Customer Story.* Before flipping the switch, HelloSpoke needs to know it works. Test scenarios cover the high-value workflows; UAT validates that real users can complete real tasks; go-live includes a hypercare window for the bumps that always show up post-launch.

*Recommended Approach.*
→ Test scenario design: top 10 sales workflows, top 5 ops workflows, top 5 reporting checks   
→ Test matrix execution with HelloSpoke: bug logging, severity triage, fix routing   
→ UAT facilitation with sales and ops users   
→ Go-live cutover and communications plan; rollback trigger   
→ 30-day hypercare: ad-hoc support, weekly check-in, fast-turn fixes for blocking issues   

*Assumptions.*
→ HelloSpoke participates in UAT (sales and ops representatives)   
→ Hypercare scope: bugs and clarifications, not net-new feature requests   
→ One cutover event; phased cutovers are change orders

#### Cross-Phase: Project Management (16 hours)

**Workstream PM: Project Management**

*Recommended Approach.*
→ Weekly standup facilitation across 12-week delivery (30 min/week)   
→ Status reporting and governance artifact maintenance (Sayer-side)   
→ Stakeholder alignment and escalation handling   
→ Phase reviews × 3: kickoff/foundation, build, UAT/go-live   

*Assumptions.*
→ Single point of contact on HelloSpoke side for status, blockers, and decisions   
→ Standing weekly meeting cadence; ad-hoc syncs available as needed

**4\. Deliverables & Milestones**

| Phase | Weeks | Milestone | Key Deliverables |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Phase 1 — Foundation + Audit | Wk 1–4 | Production-Push Green Light | HubSpot foundational architecture (objects, pipelines, lead status, lifecycle, properties, permissions); independent data QA audit + remediation; production-push green-light memo; sales-stage definitions + required-field gating at 2 handoffs; stuck-deal leadership report |
| Phase 2 — Build | Wk 5–9 | System Build Complete | Commerce Hub CPQ live (product library, quote templates, validation, approvals, native e-signature, quote-to-deal automation); DocuSign replacement decision + setup; up to 12 automations across deal-stage, lifecycle, customer-journey, ownership, alerts; 3 role-based dashboards (executive, sales rep, ops) with custom views; ClickUp workflow refinement (status alignment, gating, SOPs) |
| Phase 3 — Enable + Go-Live | Wk 10–12 | Go-Live + 30-Day Hypercare | Admin training (2 sessions, recorded); end-user training (sales + ops, recorded + live Q&A); admin playbook + end-user quick-reference cards; UAT test matrix + facilitation; go-live cutover; 30-day hypercare |
| Cross-Phase | All 12 wks | Weekly Standups + Phase Reviews | Weekly standup facilitation; status reporting; phase reviews × 3; escalation handling |

**5\. Engagement Model & Pricing** 

**Model:** Time-and-Materials with Not-to-Exceed Cap  
**Rate:** $150 per hour  
**Estimated Hours:** 164 hours across 12 weeks (10 workstreams + Project Management)  
**Not-to-Exceed Total:** $24,600  
**Payment Structure:** Four (4) equal monthly installments of $6,150  
**Terms:** Net-15  
**Technology & Administrative Fee:** 5% applied to each invoice based on consulting fees

| Phase | Workstreams | Estimated Hours | Estimated Fee |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Phase 1 — Foundation + Audit | W1, W2, W3 | 46 | $6,900 |
| Phase 2 — Build | W4, W5, W6, W7, W8 | 80 | $12,000 |
| Phase 3 — Enable + Go-Live | W9, W10 | 22 | $3,300 |
| Cross-Phase | PM | 16 | $2,400 |
| **Total** | | **164** | **$24,600** |

*Estimated Hours are based on experience with similar projects performed in the past. The client will **ONLY** be billed for actual hours worked. Detailed time sheets will be provided with invoices.* 

**6\. Project Governance**   
Sayer Responsibilities 

* Lead configuration, audit, automation build, training, and QA across all ten workstreams   
* Conduct weekly standups, status reporting, and three phase-completion reviews   
* Deliver admin playbook, end-user quick-reference cards, and recorded training sessions   
* Provide independent data QA + production-push green-light memo before any go-live cutover   
* Facilitate UAT with HelloSpoke sales + ops users; coordinate cutover and 30-day hypercare   
* Surface risks, blockers, and decision points early; manage scope and timeline proactively  

**HelloSpoke Responsibilities** 

* Designate a single primary point of contact for status, blockers, and approvals (Jeremy or designee)   
* Provide HubSpot sandbox + production access, including any required Commerce Hub Professional licensing   
* Sara to deliver finalized sales-stage definitions in week 1 of delivery   
* Make HubSpot tier and DocuSign replacement decisions on the documented timelines   
* Attend weekly standups, milestone reviews, training sessions, and UAT cycles   
* Provide product catalog (CSV), branding assets for quote templates, and any sales-quota data needed for dashboards   
* Make ClickUp-side configuration changes (Christina/Dalton/Haley); Sayer does not modify ClickUp directly

**7\. Assumptions & Constraints** 

* HelloSpoke maintains required HubSpot licenses (Sales Hub Professional minimum; Commerce Hub Professional for W4)   
* Jeremy’s sandbox build (Salesforce + Rev IO data, ClickUp integration) is functional at start of engagement; Sayer audits and validates, does not rebuild   
* All work is remote unless otherwise agreed   
* Workstream-level assumptions in Section 3 define scope boundaries for each workstream   
* Up to 2 rounds of QA findings + remediation in W2; further rounds are change orders   
* Up to 12 automations in W6; up to 3 dashboards with 5–8 reports each in W7; up to 5 document templates if PandaDoc path in W5   
* Single cutover event in W10; phased cutovers are change orders   
* Scope changes require formal written approval and may impact hours, cost, or timing   
* Estimated hours represent a not-to-exceed cap; HelloSpoke is billed for actual hours only. Any work projected to exceed the cap will be flagged in advance with a written change order before continuing

**8\. Working Agreement**

8.1 Meet with HelloSpoke staff (virtual or on location) to facilitate open lines of communication. Complete the engagement in a timely and efficient manner. Upon HelloSpoke approval, Sayer consultants may perform work remotely or from Sayer’s offices.

8.2 HelloSpoke will provide timely access to all people, files, technology, and information needed for Sayer to complete the engagement.

**9\. Payment Terms**  
9.1 Fees and Bill Rates. Fees for the Services will be invoiced in accordance with the terms listed in section five **( 5 ) Engagement Model & Pricing** — four (4) equal monthly installments of $6,150, Net-15. Invoices to be paid with acceptable payment methods of ACH, credit card or bank-to-bank transfer. Invoices which are more than 15 days past due are subject to a late charge equal to five percent (5%) of the outstanding invoice amount. Further, invoices which are more than forty-five (45) days past due shall accrue interest on the original invoice amount plus the 5% late charge at a rate of six percent (6%) per annum, or the maximum rate allowed by law, whichever is lower. Unless otherwise set forth in this Statement of Work, Our Fees for Services rendered are based on the time devoted by Sayer in the performance of the Services required. The Fees established for Sayer’s professionals are based upon the education, training, and experience of each individual. Estimated Hours are based on experience with similar projects performed in the past. The client will **ONLY** be billed for actual hours worked. Detailed time sheets will be provided with invoices. The total fee under this Statement of Work shall not exceed $24,600 without a written change order signed by both parties. 

9.2 Sayer charges a 5% fee (applied to each invoice based on consulting fees) per invoice for technology and administrative costs associated with the delivery of Services.

9.3 In the event that the Agreement or this Statement of Work is terminated prior to completion of the Services set forth in this Statement of Work, Sayer will issue a final invoice on the effective date of termination for all Services performed for which fees are outstanding.
